Note: Baseline assumptions: USD/EUR 1.08, lead times as quoted

Scenario: USD strengthens 5% (China quote in USD base)
Note: When USD strengthens, China quote gets more expensive in EUR terms; Turkey takes lead
Double digit FX move flips the ranking decisively
Turkish lira volatility is structural — buyers who quote in EUR see price cuts but supplier margin compresses and may renegotiate mid-contract
China lead time goes to 70 days. Carrying cost +0.37 per unit. Turkey's 28-day lead becomes proportionally more valuable
Poland becomes winner when tariffs escalate — note Poland was rank 5 in baseline. Diversification creates optionality value
CBAM escalation squeezes both non-EU options

Scenario: Inflation in Poland labor (supplier requests +8% price)
Note: Poland supplier remains costlier than baseline but only if Turkish & Chinese prices hold — rarely happens in practice

Scenario: All-in bad case (Red Sea + USD +10% + CBAM +€25)
Note: Combining likely 2026 stressors: China option loses its price advantage entirely

Scenario: The winner under baseline (Supplier A, China) changes under every stress scenario tested. A procurement strategy that selects Supplier A on unit price alone is fragile. A strategy that selects Supplier B (Turkey) is robust across 7 of 10 scenarios. That robustness has a cost — €0.25 per unit more than baseline — which is the insurance premium for optionality.
